person.name: Roger De Cornhill Joint Sheriff of London

nameForms.fullText: Roger De Cornhill Joint Sheriff of London

km.London, London, Middlesex, England: loនដុន, មីឌឡិសេច, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស
km.Jerusalem, Israel: מחוז ירושלים, អ៊ីស្រាអែល

zh.lifeSummary: Roger De Cornhill Joint Sheriff of London於大約1025年出生在英國米德萊可倫敦。他至少有1個兒子。他於1125年被列為joint sheriff of london。他去世於1130年在以色列מחוז ירושלים享年106歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Roger De Cornhill Joint Sheriff of London于大约1025年出生在英国米德萊可倫敦。他至少有1个儿子。他于1125年被列为joint sheriff of london。他去世于1130年在以色列מחוז ירושלים享年106岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Cornhill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name in SE England mainly from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumb) or one of the many minor places so called. It has often become   Cornell   and has sometimes been confused with   Cornwall  , and   Cornwell  , as in the case of Thomas Cornell, 1722 in   Parish Registers   (Horringer, Suffolk), who is also called Cornwall (1736), Cornwell (1740) and Cornhill (1766).
2  English:  see  Cornall  .
3  English:  see  Cornwall  .

variants.name: Cornwall
meanings: English:

habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.
variant of   Cornwell  .

variants.name: Parish Registers
meanings: English: possibly a variant of   Woodruff  . See Thomas Woodrope or Woodriffe, 1624 in   Parish Registers   (Briggate (Leeds), WR Yorks).

variants.name: Cornwell
meanings: English:
habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Cornell
meanings: English: shortened form of Cornwell , Cornwall , or of Cornhill, a habitational name from a place in Northumberland named Cornhill, from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + halh ‘nook, recess’; or from Cornhill in London, a medieval grain exchange, named with Old English corn ‘corn, grain’ + hyll ‘hill’, or from some other place elsewhere similarly named.
English: variant of   Corney  .
English: possibly also a nickname from French corneille ‘rook, crow’, probably denoting a chatterer or someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

variants.name: Cornall
meanings: 1 English: the Lancs name is a variant of Corney , where -all and -ell are hypercorrect pronunciations of Cornah, Corner, and Cornoe, late pronunciations of Corney.

2 English: the Devon name is a variant of Cornwall . Here also probably belongs Gurnall, as in Joan Gurnall, 1586 in IGI (Crediton, Devon), and William Gurnall, 1650 in IGI (Lavenham, Suffolk). Compare Corney .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: England Divided
historicEvents.description: King Canute died in 1035 and England was divided between his three sons. His son Sweyn received Norway, Harthacanute received Denmark, and Harold Harefoot received North England.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

en.lifeSummary: Roger De Cornhill Joint Sheriff of London was born about 1025, in London, Middlesex, England. He had at least 1 son. In 1125, at the age of 101, his occupation is listed as joint sheriff of london. He died in 1130, in Jerusalem, Israel, at the age of 106.

fr.lifeSummary: Roger De Cornhill Joint Sheriff of London est né environ 1025, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. En 1125, à l’âge de 101 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est joint sheriff of london. Il est décédé en 1130, à Jerusalem, Israël, à l’âge de 106 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Roger De Cornhill Joint Sheriff of London wurde um 1025 in London, Middlesex, England geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. 1125, als er 101 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als joint sheriff of london aufgeführt. Er starb 1130 im Alter von 106 Jahren in Jerusalem, Israel.

it.lifeSummary: Roger De Cornhill Joint Sheriff of London è nato il circa 1025 nel London, Middlesex, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Nel 1125, all'età di 101 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come joint sheriff of london. Morì nel 1130, nel Jerusalem, Israele, all'età di 106 anni.

ja.London, London, Middlesex, England: イギリスミッドレセックスロンドン
ja.lifeSummary: Roger De Cornhill Joint Sheriff of Londonは約1025年にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。1125年，101歳の時，職業はjoint sheriff of londonと記録されている。彼は1130年にイスラエルמחוז ירושליםで106歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger De Cornhill Joint Sheriff of London(은)는 대략 1025년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1125년, 101세의 나이로 그의 직업은 joint sheriff of london이었다. 그는 1130년 이스라엘 מחוז ירושלים에서 106세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Roger De Cornhill Joint Sheriff of London nasceu aproximadamente 1025, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Em 1125, com 101 anos, sua profissão aparece como joint sheriff of london. Ele faleceu em 1130, em Jerusalem, Israel, com 106 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Roger De Cornhill Joint Sheriff of London родился около 1025 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. В 1125 году, в возрасте 101 год,  он работал по профессии joint sheriff of london. Он умер в 1130 г. в местности מחוז ירושלים, Израиль в возрасте 106 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Roger De Cornhill Joint Sheriff of London nació aproximadamente 1025, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. En 1125, a la edad de 101 años, su ocupación registrada era joint sheriff of london. Murió en 1130, en Jerusalem, Israel, a la edad de 106 años.
